title: Material Planning and Purchasing Manager
description:
**Lincotek,** headquartered in Rubbiano, Parma, Italy, is a global contract manufacturer for services in markets including Industrial Gas Turbines, Aerospace and Medical Device applications, as well as a leading manufacturer of industrial coating equipment and one of the most respected producers in the Additive Manufacturing field. The Group has more than 1,800 employees located in 18 production facilities across Europe, North America and Asia.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

### **ABOUT THE JOB**


The selected candidate will be responsible for managing and optimizing MRP parameters within the SAP system, including minimum lot sizes, safety stock levels, lead times, and replenishment policies.


In addition, the candidate will be responsible for processing the sales forecast received from the Sales Department and translating it into a detailed forecast for each product code. They will manage, maintain, and optimize the SAP module dedicated to forecast management, monitoring deviations between customer forecasts and actual sales, and implementing the necessary corrective actions.

 **Main Responsibilities:**

* SAP MRP Parameter Management: Manage and optimize MRP parameters within the SAP system, including minimum lot sizes, safety stock levels, lead times, and replenishment policies. Ensure that parameters are properly configured to guarantee accurate planning and optimal material availability for production
* Process the sales forecast received from the Sales Department and convert it into a detailed forecast for each product code.
* Maintain, monitor and manage the SAP module dedicated to forecast management (most likely SAP IBP)
* Demand Analysis: Monitor and analyze deviations between customer forecasts and actual sales, prepare related reports, and implement the necessary adjustments within the system
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with Production, Procurement, and Logistics teams to align material planning with production schedules and customer requirements
* Analyze forecast data and material performance to identify and resolve discrepancies or issues
* Act as the key user and point of reference for the ERP forecasting module, supporting the implementation of improvements and system updates
* Management of procurement activities for raw materials, mechanical components, and subcontracted manufacturing services
* Negotiation of commercial agreements and contractual terms, including pricing, delivery schedules, and quality requirements
* Identification, evaluation, and qualification of new suppliers to support business objectives and supply chain development
* Monitoring and analysis of supplier performance through the definition and tracking of key performance indicators (KPIs) to ensure compliance with quality

 **Supervisory responsability:**


The candidate will manage the Procurement team

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**Education \& Experience**

* Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Management Engineering, Supply Chain, or a related field
* At least 5 years of previous experience in similar roles as a Material Planner and Demand Planner

 **Technical Skills**

* Strong knowledge of MRP systems and SAP, including planning modules (SAP IBP preferred)
* Excellent analytical skills and strong attention to detail
* Good command of English (B2 level or higher), both written and spoken

 **Soft Skills**

* Effective communication skills and the ability to collaborate with different business functions
* Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
* Leadership and team coordination
* Structured problem\-solving mindset

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**Work Environment**


The role operates in both an office environment and a manufacturing environment. The role routinely uses standard office equipment, such as computes, phones. The individual will be required to adhere to safety policies and procedures on the manufacturing floor.

 **Physical Demand:**


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand, walk, use hands to finger, handle or feel and reach with hands and arms.

 **Position type/Expected Hours of Work:**


Full\-time position, Monday to Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM, with flexibility for overtime based on business needs

 **Travel**


Occasional domestic and overseas travel may be required to support the rapid growth of the company

  

Lincotek provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal, or local law.

title: Senior Accounting Specialist
description:
**Location:**Bologna, BO, IT
**Job Family:** Finance
**Country/Region:** Italy  

**Sede di lavoro:** Bologna  


**L'azienda**  



Siamo a metà degli anni ’50: per la prima volta una paziente con stomia può indossare una sacca adesiva in plastica che le permette di gestire lesue necessità intime migliorando in modo significativo la sua qualità di vita.



Grazie all’intuizione di Elise Sørensen e al supporto di chi ha creduto in lei e nel suo progetto, la vita degli stomizzati in tutto il mondo cambia persempre.



Nel 1957 nasce Coloplast.


**Il mondo Coloplast**

Coloplast è oggi composta da quattro divisioni: Chronic Care, Wound \& Tissue Repair, Interventional Urology, Voice \& Respiratory Care.

  


**Chi cerchiamo**



Per il nostro team Finance siamo alla ricerca di un/a **Senior Accounting Specialist** – **Area Crediti**, che si occuperà della gestione e del controllo del ciclo attivo, contribuendo alla corretta gestione dei crediti verso clienti e al supporto delle attività di recupero.



La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno del team Finance e riporterà direttamente al Finance Manager, collaborando in maniera trasversale con diverse funzioni aziendali.



La posizione ha un ruolo chiave nel garantire l’accuratezza dei dati contabili e nel supportare il monitoraggio delle performance finanziarie legate all’area crediti.

  


**Principali Responsabilità:**


* Gestione e controllo della contabilità clienti (registrazioni, note credito, fatturazione elettronica)
* Riconciliazione incassi e monitoraggio delle partite aperte, con verifica dei conti clienti
* Supporto alle attività di credit collection, nel rispetto delle policy aziendali in materia di gestione del credito
* Collaborazione alle attività di chiusura contabile mensile e annuale
* Predisposizione della reportistica periodica (aging, DSO, esposizione crediti)
* Interfaccia con revisori interni/esterni e gestione della documentazione per eventuali azioni legali

  


**Cosa è necessario per ricoprire il ruolo:**


* Laurea in Economia, Amministrazione o discipline affini
* Esperienza di almeno 3 anni in ambito contabile, preferibilmente nell’area crediti
* Conoscenza della contabilità generale e del ciclo attivo
* Familiarità con la normativa italiana sulla fatturazione elettronica (preferenziale PA)
* Ottima conoscenza di Excel (pivot, formule avanzate)
* Familiarità con sistemi ERP (es. Oracle – preferenziale)
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese

  


**Soft skills:**


* Precisione e attenzione al dettaglio
* Ottime capacità organizzative e gestione delle scadenze
* Problem solving e gestione delle criticità
* Spiccate capacità comunicative e relazionali
* Attitudine al lavoro in team

  


**Cosa ti offriamo:**

La nostra realtà pone al centro la persona e i suoi bisogni per vocazione; cerchiamo di creare un contesto lavorativo che permetta alle nostre risorse di trovare nella nostra azienda un clima sempre accogliente e un luogo dove imparare e crescere professionalmente ed umanamente. La risorsa verrà formata e supportata nel percorso al fine di renderla autonoma sulle diverse attività potendo sempre contare sul confronto e sul supporto dei suoi colleghi per ogni necessità.


* Modalità di lavoro ibrida
* Inquadramento: Impiegato, 2° livello del CCNL Terziario, Distribuzione e Servizi;
* RAL: €37\.000 – €40\.000 lordi annui
* La retribuzione all’interno del range sarà definita in base all’esperienza e alle competenze della persona selezionata
* Bonus variabile sulla base di obiettivi assegnati
* Buoni pasto del valore di 10€

  


**Inclusion and Diversity**

Coloplast offre sempre pari opportunità di lavoro a tutti i suoi dipendenti e ai futuri candidati. Per Coloplast l’impegno verso l’inclusione e la diversità è costante e si traduce in una valutazione che esula da qualsiasi giudizio discriminatorio a causa della razza, religione, sesso, nazionalità, età, stato civile, disabilità, identità di genere e da qualsiasi altra caratteristica che non sia quella di possedere le competenze ritenute necessarie per ricoprire il ruolo.



La selezione è effettuata in conformità a quanto previsto dall’Art. 27, comma 5 del Decreto Legislativo 198/2006 – Codice delle pari opportunità.

  


*Pursuing an ambitious growth agenda, Coloplast develops and markets products and services that make life easier for people with intimate healthcare needs. Employing about 16\.000 people and with products available in more than 143 countries, we are one of the world´s leading medical device companies. We are constantly growing our business and always looking for new ways to move forward – we explore, learn and look for new ways of doing things.*  


*Coloplast is committed to being an inclusive organization, where people bring their differences to work each day, fulfil their potential and have a strong sense of belonging because – and not despite – of their differences. We therefore encourage all qualified candidates to apply regardless of gender, age, race, nationality, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religious belief or physical ability.*  


*Visit us on* *Coloplast.com**.**Watch the film.* *Follow us on* *LinkedIn**.*

61569  

\#LI\-CO
company_description: Join the team at Coloplast where our mission is to make like easier for people with intimate healthcare needs.

title: Site Regulatory Compliance Associate
description:
Overview:

We are seeking a highly organized and detail\-oriented **Site Regulatory Compliance Associate** to join our team.
The role is involved in the operational and regulatory document management of the Quality Assurance Department. Involvement in "Global" projects is also possible. He /she will report to the Change and Supplier Manager.


Main Activities \& Responsibilities:

The candidate will mainly focus on the following activities:### **1\. Site Regulatory Affairs and Liaison with Authorities**

* Management of official correspondence, GMP communications, and administrative procedures with AIFA (Italian Medicines Agency).
* Preparation, submission, and management of Regulatory Applications for the presentation of “Essential” and “Non\-Essential” changes.
* Monitoring the progress of approval procedures, ensuring compliance with company and regulatory timelines.
* Supporting the activities of the Qualified Person (QP).

### **2\. QA Compliance and Change Management**

* Evaluation and management of site Change Controls, identifying and analyzing any potential regulatory impact.
* Technical\-regulatory support in managing and evaluating Deviations and Complaints that involve compliance implications or authorization variations.
* Verifying that site activities are aligned with the Marketing Authorization Dossier requirements.

### **3\. Global Interface and Dossier Management**

* Direct collaboration and interfacing with the Global QA Regulatory Compliance department for sharing, reviewing, and updating variations to be made to Module 3 of the Registration Dossier.
* Verifying the correct flow of information between the manufacturing plant and global regulatory functions.

### **4\. Additional Activities**

* Provide support to the auditing team during internal or ecìxternal audit preparation and readiness activities related to regulatory compliance.
* Provide support in supplier qualification, process validation, CAPAs and Quality System KPIs, as well as continuous improvement activities; drafting and reviewing documentation related to the company Quality System.


Job Requirements:
* **Education:** Degree in technical/scientific disciplines (preferably CTF/Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Technology, Pharmacy, or Chemistry).
* **Languages:** Fluent written and spoken English.
* **Experience:** Experience in the regulatory sector of pharmaceutical manufacturing (at least 3 years); knowledge of main AIFA procedures and practices.
* Eligibility to act as a Qualified Person.
* **Soft Skills:**
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to interface effectively with various internal (Production, QA, Global, Regulatory Affairs) and external stakeholders.
* Strong motivation, proactivity, precision, problem\-solving attitude, strong goal\-orientation, and a "lean" approach.
* Leadership skills and assertiveness, with a determined and dynamic character.
* Good relationship skills, flexibility, and willingness to travel.
* Strong sensitivity to quality KPIs and continuous improvement topics.
* Planning and organizational skills.
* Proficiency in using spreadsheet and statistical processing software


The selected candidate will be employed under Category C in accordance with the provisions of the current National Collective Bargaining Agreement (CCNL) for the Chemical and Pharmaceutical Industry, including all applicable contractual and remuneration terms, and in compliance with Legislative Decree No. 96/2026\.

title: Plant P&O Business Partner
description:
**Company Description**  

Founded in 1825, Bormioli Pharma has been a leading manufacturer of primary packaging for the pharmaceutical industry, offering complete solutions ranging from glass and plastic bottles to plastic and aluminum closures and accessories. As of December 2024, Bormioli Pharma has been fully integrated into the Gerresheimer Group and no longer operates as a standalone brand. Today, its portfolio, expertise, and capabilities continue within Gerresheimer, an innovative systems and solutions provider and a global partner to the pharma, biotech, and cosmetics sectors. The Group offers a comprehensive range of drug containment solutions, closures and accessories, drug delivery systems, medical devices, and health\-related solutions. Gerresheimer operates more than 40 production sites across 16 countries in Europe, the Americas, and Asia, and employs approximately 13,600 people worldwide. Discover all of Gerresheimer’s innovative solutions and stay up to date by visiting Gerresheimer’s website and LinkedIn page.

 **Job Description**  

The resource will ensure the effective implementation of HR policies and processes within the manufacturing plant, supporting management and employees in the following activities:

* Ensure the application of corporate HR policies and compliance with labor legislation and company procedures
* Support the Management in industrial and union relations
* Management of recruitment, selection, and onboarding activities
* Contribute to training planning and employee development initiatives
* Monitor HR metrics and prepare related reports
* Support performance management and organizational development processes
* Promote employee engagement, welfare, and workplace culture initiatives

  

**Qualifications**  

* Degree in Economics, Law, Humanities or related disciplines
* 5/6 years of experience in roles within complex industrial and multinational environments
* Good knowledge of English language
* Analytical skills and ability to manage HR data and reporting
* Good organizational skills

  

**Additional Information**  

Availability to travel to San Vito al Tagliamento (PN) Moulded Glass Plant.


Compensation package: 40\.000€ \- 45\.000€
company_description: Gerresheimer is the global partner for pharmaceutics, biotech, healthcare, and cosmetics with a very broad product range for pharmaceutical and cosmetic packaging solutions and drug delivery systems.
